issues.title: Need to figure out how to identify devices
issues.author: rgwilton
issues.body:
Need to be able to identify sources:
- Model currently defines as source-id, and defines it as a string, but perhaps a union or more complex type is needed.
- Benoit: perhaps this is not the right place to solve this.


Also, considering  when data is configured separate and pushed up from the devices.  But still need a generic way of identifying the data.

This might be another attribute, next to the source-id...

issues.title: accuracy-measured-platinium instead of accuracy-measured-red

issues.body:
Should we replace "accuracy-measured-red" by "accuracy-measured-platinium" to mantain the logic of the next level in tiered systems (e.g., loyalty programs, credit cards, medals)?
        └── accuracy-measured
             ├── accuracy-measured-bronze
             ├── accuracy-measured-silver
             ├── accuracy-measured-gold
             ├── accuracy-measured-red <<<<<<<<< to be replaced by accuracy-measured-platinium?

issues.title: Accuracy and Precision: Dec 11 sync up call with the design team
issues.author: marisolpalmero
issues.body:
**Action**: Marisol to review documentation from vendors, on product specification on accuracy/precision, power factor implementation.

The idea is to include as a question for the working group on next Monday call:

Vendors SHOULD document the sensor type, calibration
method, and the expected measurement precision, as these factors are
distinct from accuracy but influence interpretation of the reported data?

**Action**: based on this, we need to consider to include clarification/terminology in the draft(s)

some relevant text to consider:

- Accuracy

Describes how close a measured or estimated power value is to the true physical value.

Examples:

“±10% of the actual power consumption”

“Derived from model, low fidelity”

“Direct sensor measurement, high accuracy”

- Precision

Describes the granularity or repeatability of the measurement — e.g., number of digits, stability of repeated reads.

Examples:

A sensor that reads 100.1 W, 100.2 W, 100.1 W is precise.

But if the real consumption is 120 W, the readings are precise but inaccurate.


Some more reasoning:

- A dataset may have high precision, low accuracy (stable but wrong).
- Or high accuracy, low precision (on average right, but noisy).
- Operators need accuracy for carbon reporting and power budgets.
- Engineers need precision to troubleshoot variations or detect anomalies.

Could it be something also to consider under Terminology or specific section (suggested text, to be discussed!!)
*  Accuracy versus Precision

   In the context of Power and Energy Monitoring, this document uses the
   term "accuracy" to describe how close a reported measurement is to the
   true physical value. Accuracy may be based on direct sensor readings,
   learned models, historic data, or manufacturer specifications.
   The "data-source-accuracy" identity hierarchy captures the expected
   error bounds or fidelity category of the reported value.

   While accuracy describes closeness to the true value, it is distinct
   from "precision", which refers to the repeatability, granularity, or
   number of valid digits in a measurement. A measurement may be highly
   precise yet inaccurate (e.g., stable readings that are offset from the
   real value), or accurate on average but with low precision (e.g.,
   noisy measurements).

   The accuracy identities defined in this module characterize the source
   and expected error bounds of the reported measurement but do not, by
   themselves, define the precision of the underlying sensor or data
   representation. Systems implementing this module MAY expose additional
   metadata such as sampling resolution, number of significant digits, or
   sensor calibration information to describe precision where relevant.

Based on first action, consider to add a question to the WG:
The "accuracy-measured" category indicates that the value originates from
a physical sensor. Vendors SHOULD document the sensor type, calibration
method, and the expected measurement precision, as these factors are
distinct from accuracy but influence interpretation of the reported data.

comments.author: marisolpalmero
comments.body:
here  some references to argument the discussion with the WG:
I list here the conclusions and reference:
* Most Telecom Standards Don’t Publish Accuracy
* Telecom standards (ITU, ATIS) focus on methodology and comparability across test labs.
* They assume proper calibration and good measurement practice — but do not define a fixed instrument accuracy requirement.
* Only ETSI explicitly codifies this in the measurement procedure text.
* The standards (except ETSI) do not themselves publish numeric accuracy requirements for vendor figures.



1. ETSI ES 203 136 — Lab Measurement Accuracy ONLY (NOT operational sensors)
https://www.etsi.org/deliver/etsi_es/203100_203199/203136/01.02.01_60/es_203136v010201p.pdf

 Does NOT apply to operational embedded sensors.

 Does NOT specify accuracy for device-internal energy telemetry.

________________________________

2. IETF BMWG Power Benchmarking — Benchmarking ONLY, no sensor accuracy
https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-bmwg-powerbench/

 Does NOT define accuracy or uncertainty of operational measurements.

________________________________

3. ITU-T L.1310 — Metrics and methodology, no instrument accuracy
https://www.itu.int/rec/T-REC-L.1310

Defines energy efficiency metrics & methods.

 No explicit numerical accuracy requirements.

________________________________

4. ATIS-0600015.03 — Measurement/reporting methods, no accuracy
https://shop.standards.ie/en-ie/standards/atis-0600015-03-2023-165739_saig_atis_atis_3330614/

Defines TEER metric and reporting.

 No instrument accuracy requirements.

________________________________

5. General Metrology Reference (Useful): ISO/IEC 17025
Defines calibration, uncertainty, traceability.
 Not specific to telecom equipment.

________________________________

6. Vendor Product Documentation — NONE publish measurement accuracy for embedded sensors

Cisco ISR 1000
https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/products/collateral/routers/1000-series-integrated-services-routers-isr/datasheet-c78-739512.html
 Typical/max power only. NO accuracy.

Juniper MX301
https://www.juniper.net/content/dam/www/assets/datasheets/us/en/routers/mx301-universal-routing-platform-datasheet.pdf
 No accuracy.

Juniper PTX Power Planning
https://www.juniper.net/documentation/us/en/hardware/ptx10004/topics/topic-map/ptx10004-power-planning.html
 Calculations only. No sensor accuracy.

Arista 7050
https://www.arista.com/assets/data/pdf/Datasheets/7050S_Datasheet.pdf
 “Typical power per port”. No uncertainty.

Nokia 7750 SR
https://www.nokia.com/asset/i/207452/
 Power values are simulated (HPE Power Advisor). No accuracy.

Huawei AR Series
https://e.huawei.com/en/documents/others/4b9905a27fbe49e09d1fa68cbb5d3780
 Typical/max power. No accuracy.

Only power metering products publish accuracy:
Huawei Smart Power Sensor (has accuracy class)
https://solar.huawei.com/admin/asset/v1/pro/view/10b44cb3ac91489981d76e0f752b579c.pdf

Cisco Meraki MT40 Smart Power Controller (accuracy provided)
https://documentation.meraki.com/IoT/MT_-_Sensors/Product_Information/MT_Overviews_and_Datasheets/MT40_Datasheet_-_Smart_Power_Controller

 These are external sensors, NOT embedded NIC/ASIC/PSU measuring systems.

________________________________

FINAL CONCLUSION

→ No existing telecom standard defines accuracy/uncertainty for operational embedded power sensors.

→ No major vendor publishes accuracy/uncertainty for device-internal power telemetry.

→ ETSI ES 203 136 is the only standard specifying numeric accuracy—but ONLY for external lab instruments.

→ ITU-T, ATIS, IETF do NOT specify accuracy for operational measurements.

________________________________

issues.title: Some text around the IEEE power state set

issues.body: Right now, there is just one reference to IEEE in the YANG module

issues.title: IEEE or GREEN Power State Set?
issues.author: bclaise
issues.body:
From the May 25th meeting minutes

 - Module is using powerstates based on IEEE, starts with a small initial set.
    - Suggestion:
        - Create a new IANA registry for this module.
        - Hava an IANA derived YANG module.
        - Populate with the 3 entries from IEEE 1621.
        - Keep it simple.

    - Diego: Could we also allow a device to advise IEEE values.
    - Jan: Mapping between different power states can be done but would be complex and imperfect.
    - Benoit: Don't want overkill
    - Rob: Agree to keep it simple have one small list, can extend if we need more complexity.

This would be a good topic for discussion at the IETF in Vienna

issues.title: No relationship configuration on the device-level GREEN YANG module
issues.author: bclaise
issues.body:
From the May 25th 2026 meeting minutes

- Looking at Q2: Relationship between devices.  Should they be configurable?
     -  Rob: My suggestion is no, keep relationship between devices to the controller level rather than the device level.  In case of relationship between LC and switchcard this should be populated by the device directly in the operational data.
     -  Benoit: I'm happy with this direction.
     -  General agreement between others on the call.

We should have some text around

Regards, Benoit

pulls.title: Adding "mandatory true" for current-power and its unit-multiplier
pulls.author: bclaise
pulls.body:
OLD:
module: ietf-power-and-energy
  +--ro energy-objects
     +--ro energy-entry* [object-id]
        +--ro object-id              string
        +--ro source-component-id?   -> /hw:hardware/component/name
        +--ro power
        |  +--ro current-power?          int32
        |  +--ro nameplate-power?        uint32
        |  +--ro unit-multiplier?        identityref
        |  +--ro data-source-accuracy?   identityref
        |  +--ro power-factor?           power-factor
        |  +--ro measurement-local?      boolean
        +--ro energy
           +--ro total-energy-consumed?   uint64
           +--ro total-energy-produced?   uint64
           +--ro unit-multiplier?         identityref
           +--ro data-source-accuracy?    identityref
           +--ro measurement-local?       boolean


NEW:
module: ietf-power-and-energy
  +--ro energy-objects
     +--ro energy-entry* [object-id]
        +--ro object-id              string
        +--ro source-component-id?   -> /hw:hardware/component/name
        +--ro power
        |  +--ro current-power          int32
        |  +--ro nameplate-power?        uint32
        |  +--ro unit-multiplier        identityref
        |  +--ro data-source-accuracy?   identityref
        |  +--ro power-factor?           power-factor
        |  +--ro measurement-local?      boolean
        +--ro energy
           +--ro total-energy-consumed?   uint64
           +--ro total-energy-produced?   uint64
           +--ro unit-multiplier?         identityref
           +--ro data-source-accuracy?    identityref
           +--ro measurement-local?       boolean

pulls.title: add yang relationships for review
pulls.author: williamchern
pulls.body:
add the following yang relationships
  +--ro energy-objects
     +--ro energy-entry* [object-id]
        +--ro object-id              string
        +--ro source-component-id?   -> /hw:hardware/component/name
        +--ro power
        |  +--ro instantaneous-power     int32
        |  +--ro nameplate-power?        uint32
        |  +--ro unit-multiplier         identityref
        |  +--ro data-source-accuracy?   identityref
        |  +--ro power-factor?           power-factor
        |  +--ro measurement-local?      boolean
        +--ro energy
        |  +--ro total-energy-consumed?    uint64
        |  +--ro total-energy-delivered?   uint64
        |  +--ro unit-multiplier?          identityref
        |  +--ro data-source-accuracy?     identityref
        |  +--ro measurement-local?        boolean
        +--ro energy-relationship* [relationship-id]
        |  +--ro relationship-id            string
        |  +--ro relationship-type?         identityref
        |  +--ro relationship-peer-entry* [peer-object-id]
              +--ro peer-object-id      -> /hw:hardware/component/name
              +--ro peer-description?   string

pulls.title: including IANA considerations
pulls.author: marisolpalmero

comments.author: bclaise
comments.body:
Here is a good example of what we should be doing
See https://www.iana.org/assignments/yang-parameters/yang-parameters.xhtml => iana-hardware is "Maintained by IANA?" Yes
This YANG module only contains identities. See https://www.iana.org/assignments/iana-hardware/iana-hardware.xhtml

From https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc8348/, you can see:
  The "iana-hardware" module defines YANG identities for the hardware
   types in the IANA-maintained "IANA-ENTITY-MIB" registry.

And obviously, the ietf-hardware  YANG module import iana-hardware.

We should follow the same example in our draft.

comments.author: williamchern
comments.body: It's ok for me, will add another China Energy labels to the draft after the merge. Thanks for the work.
Please clarify whether the IANA registry is a Identity Naming Registry or a Numeric Code Point Registry. It seems to be Identity Naming Registry IMO. Thanks.
thanks GenChen, you are right >>  Identity Naming Registry

```suggestion
- The proposed identity name SHOULD be a short mnemonic derived
```
fix it!

pulls.title: Power state and Reference further optimizations.
pulls.author: williamchern
pulls.body:
According to authors' discussion yesterday, the following optimizations are made following Benoit's meeting minutes:
1. Remove references to EMAN (Marisol will put the clarification in Framework draft in the same time)
2. Change the reference to power-state to IANA
3. describe in the YANG model that the oper tree will be way bigger in terms of instances than the config tree (which represent the intention)
4. have a required-instance false leafref from config tree instance to oper tree instance
5. comment in the draft: NMDA design with a single "state" leaf is also a possibiliy.

comments.author: marisolpalmero
comments.body: Please introduce a reference to NMDA >>>(Network Management Datastore Architecture,  RFC 8342)
According to Marisol's review, add reference to NMDA.

According to authors' discussion yesterday, the following optimizations are made following Benoit's meeting minutes:
1.      Remove references to EMAN (Marisol will put the clarification in Framework draft in the same time)
2.      Change the reference to power-state to IANA
3.      describe in the YANG model that the oper tree will be way bigger in terms of instances than the config tree (which represent the intent)
4.      have a required-instance false leafref from config tree instance to oper tree instance (removed because of pyang lint error)
5.      comment in the draft: NMDA design with a single "state" leaf is also a possibility.

pulls.title: YANG module design choice
pulls.author: bclaise
pulls.body: Clarified the differentiation between power-state-admin and power-state-oper YANG leaves, and explained the structure of the operational and configuration trees.

comments.author: williamchern
comments.body: Looks good, I will merge this and fix the leafref issue and publish a new version based on these changes. Thanks.

```suggestion
two leaves together form the complete power state management interface.
```

```suggestion
The operational tree ('container energy-objects') will typically contain
```

```suggestion
a significantly larger number of instances than the configuration tree
```

```suggestion
('container energy-control'). The configuration tree, which is limited to
```

```suggestion
explicitly provisioned entries, provides a compact self-contained view
of the intent. For this reason, although an NMDA (Network Management
Datastore Architecture) design with a single "state" leaf (per [RFC8342])
was considered, it is not adopted in this document.

Finally, note that the instance is in the configuration tree, have a
required-instance false leafref to operational tree instance.


```
